items.title: PSV verlengt met groeibriljant Fernandez (18): 'Dit is pas het begin'
items.summary: Noah Fernandez ziet zijn stormachtige ontwikkeling beloond worden door PSV. De achttienjarige middenvelder heeft zijn contract verlengd tot medio 2030 en sluit officieel aan bij de hoofdmacht.

items.title: Fans of richest PL clubs pay £74 per match as ticket revenue soars
items.summary: Fans of the Premier League's six wealthiest clubs are paying an average of £74 per ticket for each match they attend, while ticket revenues are soaring, according to data from a new report.

items.title: Tudor noemt degradatiestrijd met Spurs ‘lastigste uitdaging’ uit zijn loopbaan
items.summary: Igor Tudor heeft al voor veel grote klussen gestaan, maar de strijd tegen degradatie in de Premier League nog niet. Nu staat de Kroaat als interim-manager van Tottenham Hotspur voor een van de grootste uitdagingen in de clubhistorie.

items.title: Ronaldo buys 25% stake in Spanish club Almeria
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has acquired a 25% ownership stake in Spanish second division club UD Almeria.

items.title: VAR hands Atalanta winning pen after bloody foul
items.summary: Two red cards, a VAR overturn and a decisive converted penalty with the last kick of the game that came after a bloody foul on Atalanta's Nikola Krstovic.

items.title: Tchouaméni: Prestianni suspension 'victory' for all
items.summary: Real Madrid midfielder Aurélien Tchouaméni believes it was a "victory for all of us" that Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni did not feature in Wednesday's Champions League game at the Bernabéu stadium.

items.title: Handling of Man City case damaging for Premier League - Tebas
items.summary: The Premier League has been "damaged" by the fact there is yet to be a verdict in the case against Man City, says Javier Tebas.

items.title: Premier League overreactions: Spurs to get relegated?
items.summary: Let's overreact to the latest fixtures of a potentially epic Premier League season. Spurs are in dire straits.

items.title: Haaland, Rice, Semenyo ... Igor Thiago? Our writers pick Premier League Player of the Year
items.summary: We're approaching the business end of the 2025-26 Premier League season, and it's time to start thinking about year-end awards. But who has stood out? With no consensus No.1 player, our writers debate their picks.

items.title: Igor Tudor admits Spurs salvage job is biggest challenge and harder than expected
items.summary: Interim manager: ‘Now it’s a question of life and death’ Fulham clash is ‘not time to think about performance’ Igor Tudor says the salvage operation he has taken on at Tottenham is tougher than he envisaged and is most likely his hardest job in management. The interim coach, who is preparing for Sunday’s Premier League trip to Fulham, has worked with his new squad since the beginning of last week, having replaced the sacked Thomas Frank. Tudor lost his first game 4-1 at home to Arsenal on Sunday, which left Spurs four points above the relegation zone. Continue reading...

items.title: Geblesseerde Frenkie de Jong lijkt interlandperiode met Oranje niet te halen
items.summary: Barcelona moet Frenkie de Jong meerdere weken missen. De 28-jarige middenvelder heeft op de training van donderdag een hamstringblessure opgelopen. Dit heeft ook gevolgen voor de interlandperiode van maart, die lijkt De Jong namelijk niet te halen.

items.title: Heerenveen aan de slag met de scouting: 'Het is nu niet in verhouding'
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft de ambitie om Europees voetbal te halen en daarbij is het aantrekken van de juiste spelers van groot belang. Op het moment werken de Friezen echter met een bescheiden scoutingsteam. Daar moet verandering in komen.

items.title: 'The portfolio keeps expanding' - Ronaldo becomes Almeria co-owner
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes part-owner of Spanish second division side Almeria after acquiring a 25% share in the Saudi Arabian-owned club.

items.title: VI Live: AZ staat voor return in Conference League na eerdere blamage tegen FC Noah
items.summary: AZ hoopt donderdagavond de achterstand tegen FC Noah weg te poetsen. De Alkmaarders verloren in Armenië met 1-0. Of dat lukt, volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: Football Daily | Ramy Bensebaini and the stuff of nightmares in Europe for Dortmund
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Not so much a bad night at the office as a high-stakes, avant-garde masterpiece of self-destruction, Ramy Bensebaini’s performance for Borussia Dortmund as they crashed out of Bigger Cup is destined to go down in the annals as one of the most hapless in the tournament’s history. While there have been costlier mistakes (hello, Loris Karius) and far more high-profile disintegrations (bonjour, b@nter-era PSG), it is difficult to recall any one elite professional footballer being responsible for quite so many howlers in one game as the hapless Algerian left-back. Continue reading...

items.title: Champions League review: Italian struggles, Mourinho’s bus exile and a jubilant journeyman
items.summary: The knockout playoffs are complete after a whirlwind round of action. Bodø/Glimt’s fairytale continues while the holders struggled again Bodø/Glimt’s 2-1 defeat of Inter at San Siro continued this season’s miracle. The post-match discussion between Inter coach Cristian Chivu and his opposite number, Kjetil Knutsen, was one of admiration, an acknowledgment the Norwegian team had been too good for the runaway Serie A leaders. Even though Inter were without Lautaro Martínez, their standard bearer, a comeback seemed likely as they dominated the early stages. But they found no way through, eventually falling victim to the high-quality, high-speed attacking that had left them with a 3-1 deficit to overcome from the first leg. Continue reading...

items.title: Man Utd's super sub Sesko banging on door for first start under Carrick
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko has become Manchester United’s super sub after scoring three decisive goals in his last four games – but the £74m striker is banging on the door for his first start under Michael Carrick against Crystal Palace at Old Trafford on Sunday.

items.title: Senne Lammens stands tall amid the hurly-burly of United’s ‘war’ games
items.summary: The Belgian goalkeeper has quickly won over the club’s faithful but admits adapting to the Premier League’s physicality has been a challenge Senne Lammens revels in the “war” of being Manchester United’s goalkeeper in the hugely physical Premier League yet remains bemused at being applauded when entering a primary school classroom on a Wednesday afternoon. Clearly the 23-year-old is still adjusting to a rocketing career trajectory. Following his £18m transfer from Royal Antwerp on 1 September, Lammens made his debut in the 2-0 win against Sunderland on 4 October, and established himself as the first-choice with a faultless start that featured five clean sheets in 21 league games. Continue reading...

items.title: The third stage: what to expect from Emma Hayes’s USWNT with World Cup in sight?
items.summary: SheBelieves Cup campaign that starts against Argentina will show coach is now refining rather than experimenting When the whistle blows to start USA v Argentina on Sunday in Nashville, a new period of the Emma Hayes era will begin in earnest. The team preparing to play La Albiceleste in Tennessee for the 11th SheBelieves Cup, followed by Canada and Colombia, is the first in more than a year to feature no uncapped players. For a head coach who spent 2025 setting, challenging or matching all-time USWNT records for capping players, that is a notable shift and it marks the next phase of the team’s World Cup preparation. Continue reading...

items.title: A 'normal guy' doing 'brilliant' things - Lammens on life at Man Utd
items.summary: Manchester United goalkeeper Senne Lammens says he is "just a normal guy", but his reputation is growing in an impressive debut season at Old Trafford.
